title: Fit Check Call (40 min) between Sloane Barbour
meeting_title: Fit Check Call (40 min) between Leonid Shvorob and Sloane Barbour

transcript.text: What's up, Matthew?
Okay, can you hear me?
I can hear you.
Yeah, so we're waiting to slow on, or what's our plan?
I'm going to message him right now.
Okay, cool.
Okay.
Thank you.
Thank you.
Thank you.
Nice to meet you.
Yeah, thank you for jumping on the call, guys.
So how's going on on your side?
Oh, it's good.
It's very snowy here.
We're both in New York.
Yeah, sorry for you, but I didn't see snow like last three years.
Kind of missing it right now.
Where are you located?
So now I'm in Bali, Indonesia.
It's like rain season outside.
But wouldn't complain much.
Yeah, I wouldn't complain about Bali either.
Very, very snowy here.
I was just out shumbling.
Yeah, so just so you know, Matt is going to be kind of asking a lot of the questions on this call.
He's our business development and sales SDR person.
So he's been responsible for lead gen and meeting creation.
Obviously, I got an email from you or one of your colleagues.
Definitely interested in just understanding your system a bit more.
Just to be completely clear.
We're targeting primarily staffing firms.

So these are, you know, the recruiting companies themselves and the staffing firms, more so than we're targeting, you know, direct employers and enterprise employers, although we would look at enterprise employers, you know, with several thousand job listings, but most companies, we want to go through the staffing firm.
And our product is recruitment marketing and automation.

So we work with a staffing firm and integrate with Indeed, LinkedIn, ZipRecruiter, every major job board, and basically improve their candidate acquisition funnel by 30% to 40% using our AI technology.
And we also do agents, recruiting agents, AI agents, screening, scheduling, and we implement them inside of the staffing firm's workflow, integrated directly into their CRM and ATS systems.
So we have both proprietary agents and sort of customized agents that we build on behalf of our clients.
So we
That's our business.
We're doing really well.
We're doing many million dollars in sales.
We've been around for a little over three years, and we're looking to rapidly scale our meeting generation capability.
Okay, gotcha.
So you're doing all their, like, backend stuff for the sales school businesses, right?

Yeah, mean, basically, I mean, these companies are very old school, as you know, and they have pretty antiquated processes, and they work, and a lot of what recruiting involves is very human, you know, scheduling, talking on the phone, screening applicants, which historically has been difficult to automate.
And I've been in staffing for 16 years, but now AI allows you to automate and contextualize resume and job description and customer data and candidate data in a way that wasn't as easy before.
So we think it's a unique opportunity to accelerate some of these businesses in terms of revenue and profitability.
And then we can obviously capture some amount of that improvement.
Yeah, I totally got you.
So yeah, mostly I'm working also with recruitment staffing firms.
So we have also the same customers, the same market we're targeting, but I'm mostly going on to smaller firms and you're mostly going to high headcount firms as I go.
So can you share with me like what kind of staffing agencies bring you highest LTVs and slowest churn from all the market?
Yeah, so healthcare staffing firms is probably the most prominent, excuse me, is probably the most prominent in terms of how we think about to tell you,
What is my dog doing?
Sorry, my dog is chewing on something.
Healthcare staffing firms that are spending on job boards in candidate acquisition anywhere between $20,000 and $150,000 a month.
And usually this means that they have, you know, at any given time, hundreds if not thousands of jobs posted on their career page or going out to the different job boards.
That's how we kind of measure the initial opportunity for us because we're going to take over that ad spend and we're going to optimize it.
And then we're going to use that relationship to expand into, you know, building out agents and doing some of the more custom AI work that we do.
But we really land into that recruitment marketing spend.
And our co-founders and colleagues and executive team, like one of my guys, Justin, you know.
He worked in Indeed for seven years, and then he worked at ZipRecruiter for six years.
So he basically helped take ZipRecruiter public, and he ran candidate acquisition for ZipRecruiter.
So we have an incredible team when it comes to acquiring job seekers for clients.
So that's really what our expertise is.
Okay, yeah, I got you.
So you're saying that spending like $200,000, I think is $10,000 plus in annual revenue, and maybe have 10 plus recruiters?
Oh, at least 10.
Usually they have, yeah, 30, 40 or more recruiters and have...
Now, we can work with mid-market companies, like I think you're describing, like 10, 15 recruiters, you know.
We have some clients like that, but we're focused on the larger ones right now.
Yeah, makes sense.
So you're working only with the U.S.
or other countries as well?
Only U.S., only U.S.
Mm-hmm.
Okay, so...
I think market, maybe total market is 1,000 companies.
If we narrow it to healthcare, it's maybe 300, 400.
So it's not too big.
Yeah, mean, I think, well, you know, the healthcare clients that we have are spending millions of dollars a year.
So the enterprise side is really, and mid-market is probably what we're going for.
But yeah, I mean, we're not looking for like small business where there's tens of thousands.
So in the US, there's 27,000 staffing firms.
That's the data we have.
I don't know if you have different data.
And we estimate that about 10% of that, maybe 20% of that is the market we would want to go after.
So there's probably 3,000 4,000 total companies that spend over 5 or 10K a month.
But that's sort of our minimum, right?
Anything less than $10,000 a month, we're not really going to pay that much attention to.
So six-figure deals annually.
Yeah, makes sense.
So your plan is you pay proceeds for recruiters or what's the price of your service and so on?
Yes, it's based on number of job listings and how much money they want to promote their job listings.
So if they want to spend $10,000, $20,000, $50,000 a month, whatever, we're going to deploy that budget to acquire them job seekers.
So really, it depends on how many job seekers they need to acquire every month to deliver for their customers.
So if you're a healthcare staffing firm, how many nurse jobs do you have every month?
You've got 100 nurse jobs?
Well, you've probably got to spend $300 to $400 per job to get enough nurses to fill your customer demand.
So we'll spend $30,000 a month with that client.
Like we kind of...
We work with them.
They're usually already spending some amount.
Usually we get them to spend at least that.
And then we kind of scale from there.
So it's performance-based for the recruitment marketing spend.
But the AI spend for the agents and stuff is based on number of interviews that our AI does.
Stuff like that.
Usage-based.
Mm-hmm.
Okay.
Okay.
Got you.
Yeah.
So what is like your business processes right now?
How are getting this enterprise clients that you're working with?
Like inbound, like from your relationship or network or what's the...
You know, it's a combination of things.
My network, my relationships, my credibility, my LinkedIn.
Like that's been a big part of it.
Events, referrals.
So the outbound motion that Matt has been running has not converted at a very good level.
And I think this is a problem in general with outbound right now from everyone I speak to.
So we're using...
We're Apollo.
We're using HubSpot.
We're using various AI tools.
We're doing manual.
Matt's making thousands and thousands of phone calls.
And Matt has generated leads and revenue from outbound, but it's like a smaller portion of our total sales.
But I want to be able to go myself, use my network, use my LinkedIn, and use events to go after the larger mid-market and the enterprise clients.

And then I want Matt and maybe a partner like you guys to generate the higher volume sort of leads with the bigger mid-market companies and, or like healthcare providers directly, like hospital systems that like are about to post a bunch of jobs.
Like those are the kind of opportunities that we would look for you.
Like, so obviously you could, you could help us on the enterprise and do, you know, outbound or whatever it is you do.
I think I understand your system.
You know, we're, I'm very familiar with this industry.
So I've worked with Aspen Technology, you know.
And that I think there's like three or four of those companies that have like, you job listing data.
So we'd be curious, like how you would attack this and what sort of that looks like and what your experience is specifically trying to generate leads at staffing firms.
So it seems like you generate leads for staffing firms, which means probably know a lot of staffing firms, which means maybe you want to just do referrals for us and I'll pay you 10% or whatever.
But I think, you know, we would like to see how much you, you know, what exactly you do here.
Yeah, makes sense.
So you mentioned that you also want to target healthcare providers.
So like, so your service can be used not only for staffing firms, but for like, healthcare, like clinics and so on, who do it without recruiters, but their internal teams?
Yeah, we do we work with both direct employers and staffing firms, but direct employers is a lower priority.
Mm-hmm.
Yeah, we go.
Yeah, Yeah, go.
We have both customers, but about 80% of our revenue is staffing.
Okay, okay.
So you also targeting this like hospitals and some like clinics who doing hiring themselves or now your main focus on staffing agencies for like bigger sizes, as you mentioned, right?
Big staffing agencies.
Okay, okay.
Yeah, say that Matthew and your team tried some outreach that didn't convert much.
If you share with me what you tried, what not, I can compare with strategies that I use that I will uncover later.
So just to see if it makes sense.
Yeah, so we use Apollo.
And I mean, you could probably speak a bit more to some of the numbers, but I think the conversion rates are less than 1%, I mean, into meetings and stuff.
Yeah, not really getting a lot of opens either, which is one of bigger issues.
We're doing some A-B testing on those different subject lines, and just not getting the open.
So not sure if it's just a messaging thing, a subject line thing, or just going to spam and not getting opened.
Yes, this.
Okay, so you're doing just blasting, spraying to Apollo leads?
No.
And what's like their process?
We're not doing the spraying, we're doing the targeted outreach, the targeted messaging, two different personas.
So having a bunch of different stuff running at the same time.
it's not spraying, it's really going to target messaging.
Yeah.
So yeah, basically it's most about quality, what's like signals you got.
So you're doing signal-based when you've got some signals, for example, so this staffing firm needs, like they're expanding, or they have more job boards, jobs.
So the list set that I built out, it already has what they're spending a year.
So that's kind of, signal is baked into it.
So we're trying to use some Apollo signals to kind of get more traction, but using the list that we build out.
And the companies that we're reaching out to, they're already spending the minimum amount of money that other companies we want to work with.
So that kind of signal is already baked in, and they already have jobs that they're posting, which we know.
Those two signals are baked into the list that I built out.
Yeah, so we have, obviously, Apollo incorporates job listing data, but for an example, one of the things that we did for, you know, like, we do LinkedIn advertising.

So we'll run ads on LinkedIn, we'll see who comes to our website and who engages with those ads and what our top engaged customers or prospects are, and then we'll run targeted campaigns against those customer names.
So we're doing, like, account-based marketing on the enterprise.
When we're doing an event, Matt will do a bunch of outreach for event meetings, which has worked really well because I think it's, like, a catalyst of, like, one thing.
But, yeah, would say just the general, like, hey, these 100 companies are mid-market, and they're posting between 50 and 150 jobs a month.
Let's do
Some prospecting to them via Apollo, we're not really seeing anything like material come back from that.
And as Matt said, I mean, we have SendGrid, we've warmed up multiple inboxes, we're sending from like six different emails.
Like, I feel like we've set up everything correctly, but I could be wrong.
Yeah, yeah, yeah.

I think I know what's one of the issues you're facing because in terms of how the emails should place into mailboxes, of recruiters, and this is a very old-school industry, and especially for enterprise, the main issue, their message was delivered.
Because they all have Microsoft providers, and now it's very tricky to land it to inbox.
So we have the whole process around it.
So this is first.
So yeah, guess this, and also like about the quality of copy and so on.
This is second, based on what signals you get.
you.
Thank

Because only like getting the signals that they spend in X amount of money, usually this kind of signals this okay, but what works for clients that I work in, so we're trying to make in more like not obvious signals, that can be so this like companies change like leadership, or they just change of hiring volume or something.

So based, like based on targets, based on the goal, we're making the list of signals and going like quarterly, like main messages, like checking in, what's going on, what's up, and if we around this time get more specific signals, we double down on that.
For example, they like hire new ops leaders, so on, it's usually for you guys, I think makes sense that they with.

Be more receptive to your service, so this as well, and also how you mentioned that events work for you, so I think in this case it's good to monitoring some very niche events, especially for staffing firms, and just keeping some signals from there.

And also in terms of kind of those new people who are starting jobs, like I'm using LinkedIn sales and have a lot to really get granular in that, in terms of who's posted within the past 30 days, who's changed jobs in the last 90 days, so really kind of using those signals as well, and then transferring them over to Apollo, so getting granular there, using the account list that we're going after.

You know, I've been running outbound and SDR teams for, I mean ever, for since 2006, we've been doing outbound and various types, you know, outreach when it first came out was amazing, and then it got bad because everyone got it, and then, you know.
Spam rules get tight.
Like there's obviously always evolving issues you have to face.
And my wife works for Salesforce and she knows a lot about, you know, deliverability and whatnot.
And I, we've definitely heard that Microsoft and Microsoft inbox controls, you know, bounce rate, delivery rates are very low, bounce rates are very high.
So, I mean, what's special about your system that allows you to inbox people that have Microsoft that we couldn't just do with Apollo?
Like, why can't Apollo do that, given that they're one of the leaders in the space?
Yeah, that's a good question.
If I just tell you all my secrets straight away, it wouldn't make much sense for me, right?
But on the, like, base level, what we're doing, we're tracking, deliverability across providers.
So, when we launch campaign, we say, okay, for Google, this is our baseline, how many, like, replies we have, so we can...
Pairing with Microsoft, if we see lower rates, we see if it's landing or not, if it's not landing, we do it again, again, again, until it's landing.

So it's a lot of stuff from here, and if you're just using Apollo, they don't have this kind of analytics, so it's good just to, I don't know, touch the water, but for, like, long-term game, as you say, if you want to scale, I think you should know when your message getting into inbox, when it's not, so you can, like, tweak in your angles, different, like, copies, like, value prepositions.
So I think for you, if you don't crack there, these points, because, like, 80%, I think for enterprise is, like, 90% is Microsoft mailboxes.
So kind of like this.
Yeah, and Lena, do you have a, so when we work with you, or if we were.
We're work with you.
What is the arrangement in terms of how we understand the analytics of what you're delivering?

You know, how do we, do we basically, because I've worked with third-party lead generation services, so this is not that new to me, but typically we'll give them, you know, a bunch of copy, target list, ICP, all of that.
You'll create the campaigns, you'll start doing, you know, outbound, you'll run it from multiple warmed up email addresses.
Are you emailing from our domain?
Do you set up your own domains?
You know, how does it work?
Mm-hmm.
Okay, yeah.

So, usually, what you're saying, there's, like, I don't know, maybe lead agency playbook, where you're just giving a bunch of, like, your playbooks for them, your rules, and they're doing just technical stuff.

That's not how I'm working with clients, because I'm about business development, so I, what we're doing when we have onboarding new clients, we're seeing what we can do, we have our copy that's working.
So...
So...
So at least we're trying different strategies.
For example, we can create accounts under your name or we can use our infrastructure and reach now to the center price and say, okay, we know this.
I have some guys that helps you reduce costs on your hiring.
Do you want to reconnect you too?
So try different angles and this kind of stuff.
So how are tracking all these metrics?
If it's important for you, just we can clients access all their numbers.
So also you can share like what numbers working for you, what outreach strategies you already try, what works, what not.
We can take a couple of these because of course you understand your market better, at least for now in outreach.
Yes.
And it makes sense to try a couple of these.
So, but usually all their infrastructure, all their mapping signals.
All their internal systems that we use to identify their people, in your case, like OPS leaders and who are working in this kind of healthcare staffing agencies when something's changing.
So this I prefer doing myself.
Got it.
So do you charge per lead generated?
Is it a monthly fee?
Is it a retainer?
What's your cost structure?

So yeah, usually when I'm working with my lovely recruiters, it's like a 90-day pilot, because what lasts, like if just one month or something, it doesn't make sense, because to make the system running, it takes some time.
So for you, I think it can be more than 90 days, but yeah, 90 days is a minimal baseline.
In terms of generating opportunities, usually, it's if it's more...
Or like smaller businesses, more commodity.
Like if I work in for healthcare recruitment itself, I would say that I can connect them with like at least five decision makers per month.
But for you, because you're targeting more enterprise clients, I would say that like three, five good opportunities, good conversations that I make for you for like first 90 days, it will be good.
Okay.
So we do a 90 day pilot with our customers.
I like that arrangement.
I agree.
We're looking to invest for some time.
We would be looking, look, we're doing a ton of our own campaigns, obviously.
We're doing marketing, we're doing outbound, we're doing this.
So we'd be looking for this as like a supplemental, like if we can generate, you know, 20 or 30% of our lead base from a partner like you, that would be great.
Right now our close rate.
is very high.
We're closing like 30%.
35% to 40% of every person we talk to, that's a qualified opportunity, which probably needs any to raise my prices.
But we definitely have very good success.
We know how to qualify.
We know exactly what we're looking for.
We can give very specific feedback on lead quality.

But yeah, I mean, if you're seeing, if you have these signals on job listings and you're able to identify, hey, this company went from 30 to 50 job listings a month on average in the last three months, that's probably a strong signal that there's growth and that they're going to be investing heavily in this category, right?
So it sounds like, you know, they hire a new VP of marketing.
They hire a new head of recruiting operations.
They hire a new CMO, right?
They get a new executive on the team who wants to shake things up.
But the people we sell to, Leonid, like in a staffing firm, it's almost always VP marketing or chief marketing officer because usually this sits under marketing.
So it's probably the same person you sell to in staffing firms, which means that.
You could just start to make referrals and get paid, which would be totally fine by us.
However you get the leads doesn't matter.
Maybe they're existing customers of yours.
And then the other person that we see inside of a staffing firm is like recruiting operations manager, head of recruiting ops, director of recruiting, VP recruiting, chief operating officer.
So usually it's kind of like somehow both of those people.
And then once we get in, of course, the CTO, the head of product, like we're working with them on the AI implementations, but we're not really going in through the CTO because they usually don't pay.
They are not like responsible for the marketing to get candidates.
And so that's kind of why we focus more on the VP marketing.
Yeah, makes sense.
So I would first targeting all ops roles, but if you're saying for you marketing doing also good as well, yeah, it's a good signal.
So, in terms of singles, uh, that you mentioned, uh, what we do in these cases.
So we just, uh,

We the chunk of markets, as you say, like 3K firms, and then we annually monitoring them like every one month, sometimes every week, and we see changes in their job boards, in their website, LinkedIn, and when we see something change, we analyze what change, if it's change looks like a signal, we're reaching out.
So basically like this.
And what would we expect if we were to work together in terms of a monthly budget we would put towards this?
So for clients I worked before with SaaS, I charged from 5K to 7K.
So basically like this, because it's more system-heavy to make all this infrastructure running.
So we can make another call when I make just proposals, when I make more info, what signals we're tracking, and I will see about.
I'll look more deep in your markets and I can finalize the price, but usually it's from $5,000 to $7,000.
Got it.
So that's fine.
So can you do me a favor?
Can we please take a look at that proposal?
We're talking to a few different partners here.
We're going to make a decision by the end of the month on this.
We're going to try to get launched as soon as possible.
So yeah, I would love to see what that proposal looks like.
And if you have specific experience working with like software companies or marketing, recruitment marketing companies like us, selling to staffing firms, obviously that would be fantastic.
Yeah.
So I know when they're sending proposals, they usually get buried.
So if you have someone from your team that also want to join for our next call, we can schedule it.
Like you say, you want to make the decision by the end of the month.
It's already last week.
It's
Yeah, no, I think by the – you're right.
I thought it was actually – I'm just looking at my calendar.
We're probably – based on the calls we have coming up, we're probably not going to be able to get to that.
So it's probably going to have to be by mid-February.
But let me see here.
I'm looking at my calendar.
Yeah, I'd like to do – if we could do another call like Monday, same time on Monday, February 2nd.
Matt, if that works for you, I think that would be good.
Mm-hmm.
Okay, that was great.
I will prepare all the info that we're talking and also some cases with like staffing agencies and one couple of like SaaSs.
I worked.
So if you wanted to take a look and from there we can make a decision if it makes sense to work together or not.
Yeah, absolutely.
Okay.
So thank you, guys.
I will send you calendar invitation.
Thank
Today, tomorrow, and I guess see you 2nd February, unless you have some questions for me right now.
No, we're good.
This is helpful, and we get it, and this is pretty standard.
We're just looking for the right partner, so yeah, we'll look forward to seeing that proposal.
Okay, Sloane, Matthew, have a good rest of your day.
Thanks.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Evaluate SystemHustle as a partner for scaling engin's meeting generation.](https://fathom.video/share/mmHYyezSE9mb1pzkF_G_AfsLXzXdueLG?tab=summary&timestamp=171.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - **Problem:** engin's outbound outreach (Apollo) has \<1% conversion, blocked by poor email deliverability to the staffing industry's dominant Microsoft inboxes.
  - [**Solution:** SystemHustle proposes a signal-based strategy to bypass deliverability issues and target high-intent prospects (e.g., firms with sudden job-post growth), aiming for 3-5 qualified meetings in a 90-day pilot.](https://fathom.video/share/mmHYyezSE9mb1pzkF_G_AfsLXzXdueLG?tab=summary&timestamp=980.0)
  - [**Opportunity:** engin needs to scale meeting volume to supplement its high-converting inbound channels (35-40% close rate) and capture a market of \~3,000–4,000 target firms.](https://fathom.video/share/mmHYyezSE9mb1pzkF_G_AfsLXzXdueLG?tab=summary&timestamp=1476.0)
  - [**Decision:** engin will review a formal proposal from SystemHustle on **Feb 2nd** to decide on a partnership by mid-February.](https://fathom.video/share/mmHYyezSE9mb1pzkF_G_AfsLXzXdueLG?tab=summary&timestamp=1694.0)

## Topics

### engin's Business & Market

  - [**Product:** AI-powered recruitment marketing & automation for staffing firms.](https://fathom.video/share/mmHYyezSE9mb1pzkF_G_AfsLXzXdueLG?tab=summary&timestamp=179.0)
      - [**Goal:** Improve candidate acquisition funnels by 30-40%.](https://fathom.video/share/mmHYyezSE9mb1pzkF_G_AfsLXzXdueLG?tab=summary&timestamp=203.0)
      - [**Services:** AI agents for screening/scheduling, integrated with client CRMs/ATSs.](https://fathom.video/share/mmHYyezSE9mb1pzkF_G_AfsLXzXdueLG?tab=summary&timestamp=203.0)
  - [**Target Market:** US staffing firms, with a focus on healthcare.](https://fathom.video/share/mmHYyezSE9mb1pzkF_G_AfsLXzXdueLG?tab=summary&timestamp=329.0)
      - [**Ideal Client Profile (ICP):** Firms spending $20k–$150k/mo on job boards, typically with 30-40+ recruiters.](https://fathom.video/share/mmHYyezSE9mb1pzkF_G_AfsLXzXdueLG?tab=summary&timestamp=329.0)
      - [**Market Size:** \~3,000–4,000 firms spending \>$10k/mo on job boards.](https://fathom.video/share/mmHYyezSE9mb1pzkF_G_AfsLXzXdueLG?tab=summary&timestamp=516.0)
  - [**Sales Motion:**](https://fathom.video/share/mmHYyezSE9mb1pzkF_G_AfsLXzXdueLG?tab=summary&timestamp=625.0)
      - [**Inbound (High-Converting):** Sloane's network, events, referrals.](https://fathom.video/share/mmHYyezSE9mb1pzkF_G_AfsLXzXdueLG?tab=summary&timestamp=625.0)
      - [**Outbound (Low-Converting):** Matthew's Apollo-based campaigns.](https://fathom.video/share/mmHYyezSE9mb1pzkF_G_AfsLXzXdueLG?tab=summary&timestamp=807.0)
  - [**Sales Process:**](https://fathom.video/share/mmHYyezSE9mb1pzkF_G_AfsLXzXdueLG?tab=summary&timestamp=1546.0)
      - [**Target Personas:** VP Marketing/CMO (budget holder), Head of Recruiting Ops (user).](https://fathom.video/share/mmHYyezSE9mb1pzkF_G_AfsLXzXdueLG?tab=summary&timestamp=1546.0)
      - [**Close Rate:** 35-40% on qualified opportunities.](https://fathom.video/share/mmHYyezSE9mb1pzkF_G_AfsLXzXdueLG?tab=summary&timestamp=1496.0)

### Outbound Performance & Challenges

  - [**Current Strategy:** Targeted campaigns via Apollo and HubSpot, not "spraying."](https://fathom.video/share/mmHYyezSE9mb1pzkF_G_AfsLXzXdueLG?tab=summary&timestamp=857.0)
  - **[Performance:](https://fathom.video/share/mmHYyezSE9mb1pzkF_G_AfsLXzXdueLG?tab=summary&timestamp=807.0)** \<1% conversion to meetings; low open rates.
  - [**Root Cause:** Poor email deliverability to the staffing industry's dominant Microsoft inboxes.](https://fathom.video/share/mmHYyezSE9mb1pzkF_G_AfsLXzXdueLG?tab=summary&timestamp=980.0)
      - [**Impact:** Messages are likely sent to spam, preventing opens and conversions.](https://fathom.video/share/mmHYyezSE9mb1pzkF_G_AfsLXzXdueLG?tab=summary&timestamp=841.0)

### SystemHustle's Proposed Solution

  - [**Core Insight:** The staffing industry's reliance on Microsoft inboxes requires a specialized deliverability strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/mmHYyezSE9mb1pzkF_G_AfsLXzXdueLG?tab=summary&timestamp=980.0)
  - [**Proposed Strategy:**](https://fathom.video/share/mmHYyezSE9mb1pzkF_G_AfsLXzXdueLG?tab=summary&timestamp=980.0)
      - [**Deliverability Optimization:** Continuously test and adjust campaigns to ensure inbox placement on Microsoft domains.](https://fathom.video/share/mmHYyezSE9mb1pzkF_G_AfsLXzXdueLG?tab=summary&timestamp=1176.0)
      - [**Signal-Based Targeting:** Monitor target firms for high-intent signals beyond basic spend data.](https://fathom.video/share/mmHYyezSE9mb1pzkF_G_AfsLXzXdueLG?tab=summary&timestamp=1010.0)
